name: Rob Roy

spec.method_detail: Stir with ice until well-chilled. Strain into chilled coupe.

history: A Manhattan made with Scotch. Named for Robert Roy MacGregor, a Scottish folk hero, on the occasion of an operetta about him opening at the Herald Square Theatre in New York in 1894. The drink commemorated the production. It's a perfect example of how cocktail names capture cultural moments.
